name: Garlic Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the garlic and vinegar in a 2-quart kettle.
2. Simmer the mixture gently, uncovered, over medium heat for 15 minutes.
3. Remove the pan from the heat and pour the mixture into a 1-quart glass jar.
4. Cover the jar and let it stand at room temperature for 24-36 hours.
5. Pour the flavored vinegar through a wire strainer into a bowl, pressing the garlic with the back of a spoon to squeeze out liquid. Discard any residue.
6. Measure the liquid and add vinegar if needed to make 1 cup.
7. Measure the sugar into a dry bowl.
8. Combine the garlic-vinegar solution and water in a 5- or 6-quart kettle.
9. Add pectin, stirring well.
10. Over high heat, bring the mixture to a boil, stirring constantly to avoid scorching.
11. Add sugar, and stir well. Bring the mixture to a full, rolling boil.
12. Add butter to reduce foaming. Continue stirring.
13. Boil the mixture hard for exactly 2 minutes.
14. Remove the pan from the heat and skim off any foam.
15. Add red, yellow, or orange food coloring if desired.
16. Pour the jelly into prepared glasses. Seal according to directions on the recipe folder in the pectin package.
17. Makes approximately 5 cups.
